revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=櫻井子安神社 |画像=[[File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|280px|櫻井子安神社　千葉県旭市]] |所在地=千葉県旭市桜井1264 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[木花咲耶姫命]]
[[伊邪那岐命]]
[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|櫻井子安神社　手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[木花咲耶姫命]] [[伊邪那岐命]] [[伊邪那美命]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == 当神社は、祭神に木花咲耶姫命(このはなさくやひめのみこと)、伊邪那岐命(いざなぎのみこと)、伊邪那美命(いざなみのみこと)を奉齋しております。 勧請の年月は、詳らかではありませんが、極めて古くに此の地に祀られました。 古来当神社は、出産は勿論、なかなか子宝に恵まれない婦人の信心宿願あれば顕著な霊験があるとして、遠くからも陸続と参詣者があり、上代櫻井(古き土地の名称)の子安さまと尊称されていました。また、古くは櫻井子安大神と呼ばれております。 (ホームページより引用) == 境内 == == 文化財 == == 行事 == == 交通 == == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [http://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト）

{{神社 |名称=櫻井子安神社 |画像=[[File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|280px|櫻井子安神社　千葉県旭市]] |所在地=千葉県旭市桜井1264 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[木花咲耶姫命]]
[[伊邪那岐命]]
[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|櫻井子安神社　手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[木花咲耶姫命]] [[伊邪那岐命]] [[伊邪那美命]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == 当神社は、祭神に木花咲耶姫命(このはなさくやひめのみこと)、伊邪那岐命(いざなぎのみこと)、伊邪那美命(いざなみのみこと)を奉齋しております。 勧請の年月は、詳らかではありませんが、極めて古くに此の地に祀られました。 古来当神社は、出産は勿論、なかなか子宝に恵まれない婦人の信心宿願あれば顕著な霊験があるとして、遠くからも陸続と参詣者があり、上代櫻井(古き土地の名称)の子安さまと尊称されていました。また、古くは櫻井子安大神と呼ばれております。 (ホームページより引用) == 境内 == == 文化財 == == 行事 == == 交通 == JR総武本線　旭駅 → バス停萬歳小学校 下車 徒歩11分 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [http://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト）

[[伊邪那岐命]]
[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|櫻井子安神社　手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[木花咲耶姫命]] [[伊邪那岐命]] [[伊邪那美命]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == == 境内 == == 文化財 == == 行事 == == 交通 == JR総武本線　旭駅 → バス停萬歳小学校 下車 徒歩11分 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [http://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト） {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:さくらいこやすしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

{{Cleanup|date=2015年3月}} {{神社 |名称=櫻井子安神社 |画像=[[File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|280px|櫻井子安神社　千葉県旭市]] |所在地=千葉県旭市桜井1264 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[木花咲耶姫命]]
[[伊邪那岐命]]
[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|櫻井子安神社　手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[木花咲耶姫命]] [[伊邪那岐命]] [[伊邪那美命]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == 当神社は、宮司を務める上代家の本務社である「左右大神」の境外社として櫻井の地に鎮座しており、御祭神に[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]](このはなさくやひめのみこと)、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]](いざなぎのみこと)、[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]](いざなみのみこと)を奉齋しております。 勧請の年月は、詳らかではありませんが、極めて古くに此の地に祀られました。 古来当神社は、お祀りしている御祭神からもお分かりのとおり良縁を求める人々の縁結びのご利益、出産は勿論、なかなか子宝に恵まれない婦人の信心宿願あれば顕著な霊験があるとして、遠くからも陸続と参詣者があり、上代郷櫻井(古き土地の名称)の子安さまと尊称されていました。また、古くは櫻井子安大神と呼ばれております。 口伝によれば、当社の赤麻紐(子安さまの赤帯)は、男女の縁は固より親子の縁をも繋ぐと云われ、良縁を望む子女、子宝に恵まれない婦人は、懐中に抱くことによりその願いが叶うと伝えられています。 後に時間と共に、この赤麻紐は、良縁、子宝安産の他にも健康、学業、開運等の御利益もあるとお守りとして人々に大切にされております。 また、当神社の鎮座します地域は、[[神葬祭]](神道式の葬儀)を行う家庭もあるということから、命を授かり誕生し、[[七五三]]を経て、成長の過程での儀礼、[[厄祓い]]、健康長寿の祈願、そして実りあった一生を締めくくる[[神葬祭]]と、子安さまは、人の一生を見守ってくださる神様としても大変貴ばれております。 == 境内 == 御神木、手水舎、社殿、石碑、社務所がある。 == 文化財 == == 行事 == 1月1日　歳旦祭 毎月1日、15日　月次祭 9月　氏神祭 11月　例祭 == 交通 == JR総武本線　旭駅 → バス停萬歳小学校 下車 徒歩11分 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [https://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト） {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:さくらいこやすしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

{{複数の問題 |Cleanup=2015年3月 |出典の明記=2018-10}} {{神社 |名称=櫻井子安神社 |画像=[[File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|280px|櫻井子安神社　千葉県旭市]] |所在地=千葉県旭市桜井1264 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[木花咲耶姫命]]
[[伊邪那岐命]]
[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|櫻井子安神社　手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[木花咲耶姫命]] [[伊邪那岐命]] [[伊邪那美命]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == 当神社は、宮司を務める上代家の本務社である「左右大神」の境外社として櫻井の地に鎮座しており、御祭神に[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]](このはなさくやひめのみこと)、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]](いざなぎのみこと)、[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]](いざなみのみこと)を奉齋しております。 勧請の年月は、詳らかではありませんが、極めて古くに此の地に祀られました。 古来当神社は、お祀りしている御祭神からもお分かりのとおり良縁を求める人々の縁結びのご利益、出産は勿論、なかなか子宝に恵まれない婦人の信心宿願あれば顕著な霊験があるとして、遠くからも陸続と参詣者があり、上代郷櫻井(古き土地の名称)の子安さまと尊称されていました。また、古くは櫻井子安大神と呼ばれております。 口伝によれば、当社の赤麻紐(子安さまの赤帯)は、男女の縁は固より親子の縁をも繋ぐと云われ、良縁を望む子女、子宝に恵まれない婦人は、懐中に抱くことによりその願いが叶うと伝えられています。 後に時間と共に、この赤麻紐は、良縁、子宝安産の他にも健康、学業、開運等の御利益もあるとお守りとして人々に大切にされております。 また、当神社の鎮座します地域は、[[神葬祭]](神道式の葬儀)を行う家庭もあるということから、命を授かり誕生し、[[七五三]]を経て、成長の過程での儀礼、[[厄祓い]]、健康長寿の祈願、そして実りあった一生を締めくくる[[神葬祭]]と、子安さまは、人の一生を見守ってくださる神様としても大変貴ばれております。 == 境内 == 御神木、手水舎、社殿、石碑、社務所がある。 == 文化財 == == 行事 == 1月1日　歳旦祭 毎月1日、15日　月次祭 9月　氏神祭 11月　例祭 == 交通 == JR総武本線　旭駅 → バス停萬歳小学校 下車 徒歩11分 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [https://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト） {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:さくらいこやすしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

{{出典の明記|date=2018-10}} {{神社 |名称=櫻井子安神社 |画像=[[File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|280px|櫻井子安神社　千葉県旭市]] |所在地=千葉県旭市桜井1264 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[木花咲耶姫命]]
[[伊邪那岐命]]
[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|櫻井子安神社　手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]。社殿前には縁起物「赤麻紐」が中に入った赤い木の玉の「祈願玉」が置いてある。[https://spicomi.net/media/articles/3310 櫻井子安神社の徹底取材レポ！ご利益・御朱印・お守り] == 祭神 == [[木花咲耶姫命]] [[伊邪那岐命]] [[伊邪那美命]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == 当神社は、宮司を務める上代家の本務社である「[[左右大神]]」の境外社として櫻井の地に鎮座しており、御祭神に[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]](このはなさくやひめのみこと)、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]](いざなぎのみこと)、[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]](いざなみのみこと)を奉齋しております。 勧請の年月は、詳らかではありませんが、極めて古くに此の地に祀られました。 古来当神社は、お祀りしている御祭神からもお分かりのとおり良縁を求める人々の縁結びのご利益、出産は勿論、なかなか子宝に恵まれない婦人の信心宿願あれば顕著な霊験があるとして、遠くからも陸続と参詣者があり、上代郷櫻井(古き土地の名称)の子安さまと尊称されていました。また、古くは櫻井子安大神と呼ばれております。 口伝によれば、当社の赤麻紐(子安さまの赤帯)は、男女の縁は固より親子の縁をも繋ぐと云われ、良縁を望む子女、子宝に恵まれない婦人は、懐中に抱くことによりその願いが叶うと伝えられています。 後に時間と共に、この赤麻紐は、良縁、子宝安産の他にも健康、学業、開運等の御利益もあるとお守りとして人々に大切にされております。 また、当神社の鎮座します地域は、[[神葬祭]](神道式の葬儀)を行う家庭もあるということから、命を授かり誕生し、[[七五三]]を経て、成長の過程での儀礼、[[厄祓い]]、健康長寿の祈願、そして実りあった一生を締めくくる[[神葬祭]]と、子安さまは、人の一生を見守ってくださる神様としても大変貴ばれております。 == 境内 == 御神木、手水舎、社殿、石碑、社務所がある。 == 文化財 == == 行事 == 1月1日　歳旦祭 毎月1日、15日　月次祭 9月　氏神祭 11月　例祭 == 交通 == JR総武本線　旭駅 → バス停萬歳小学校 下車 徒歩11分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [https://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト） {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:さくらいこやすしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|250px|手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]桜井にある[[神社]]。社殿前には縁起物「赤麻紐」が中に入った赤い木の玉の「祈願玉」が置いてある。[https://spicomi.net/media/articles/3310 櫻井子安神社の徹底取材レポ！ご利益・御朱印・お守り] == 歴史 == 当神社は、宮司を務める上代家の本務社である「[[左右大神]]」の境外社として櫻井の地に鎮座しており、御祭神に[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]](このはなさくやひめのみこと)、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]](いざなぎのみこと)、[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]](いざなみのみこと)を奉齋しております。 勧請の年月は、詳らかではありませんが、極めて古くに此の地に祀られました。 古来当神社は、お祀りしている御祭神からもお分かりのとおり良縁を求める人々の縁結びのご利益、出産は勿論、なかなか子宝に恵まれない婦人の信心宿願あれば顕著な霊験があるとして、遠くからも陸続と参詣者があり、上代郷櫻井(古き土地の名称)の子安さまと尊称されていました。また、古くは櫻井子安大神と呼ばれております。 口伝によれば、当社の赤麻紐(子安さまの赤帯)は、男女の縁は固より親子の縁をも繋ぐと云われ、良縁を望む子女、子宝に恵まれない婦人は、懐中に抱くことによりその願いが叶うと伝えられています。 後に時間と共に、この赤麻紐は、良縁、子宝安産の他にも健康、学業、開運等の御利益もあるとお守りとして人々に大切にされております。 また、当神社の鎮座します地域は、[[神葬祭]](神道式の葬儀)を行う家庭もあるということから、命を授かり誕生し、[[七五三]]を経て、成長の過程での儀礼、[[厄祓い]]、健康長寿の祈願、そして実りあった一生を締めくくる[[神葬祭]]と、子安さまは、人の一生を見守ってくださる神様としても大変貴ばれております。 == 祭神 == * [[木花咲耶姫命]] * [[伊邪那岐命]] * [[伊邪那美命]] == 境内 == * 社殿 * 社務所 * 御神木 * 手水舎 * 石碑 == 行事 == * 1月1日 - 歳旦祭 * 9月 - 氏神祭 * 11月 - 例祭 * 毎月1日、15日 - 月次祭 == 交通 == JR総武本線　旭駅 → バス停萬歳小学校 下車 徒歩11分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [https://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト） {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:さくらいこやすしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to deities associated with [[safe childbirth]] and [[child-rearing]]. Shrines are generally divided into those enshrining [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], who is also the deity of [[Mount Fuji]] (and the main deity of [[Sengen Shrine]]), and those enshrining [[Kushinadahime]], the wife of [[Susanoo]]. Both deities are genealogically related, with the former being the child and the latter being..."
revision.text:
'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to deities associated with [[safe childbirth]] and [[child-rearing]].
Shrines are generally divided into those enshrining [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], who is also the deity of [[Mount Fuji]] (and the main deity of [[Sengen Shrine]]), and those enshrining [[Kushinadahime]], the wife of [[Susanoo]]. Both deities are genealogically related, with the former being the child and the latter being the grandchild of [[Ōyamatsumi]].
== Kanto Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Mito)|ja|子安神社 (水戸市)}} - Located in Chidami, Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kasumigaura)|ja|子安神社 (かすみがうら市)}} - Located in Higashinoji, Kasumigaura City, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Tone)|ja|子安神社 (利根町)}} - Located in Obusa, Tone Town, Kita-Soma District, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|ja|櫻井子安神社}} - Located in Sakurai, Asahi City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Sosa)|ja|子安神社 (匝瑳市)}} - Located in Yokoshiba, Sosa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hanamigawa)|ja|子安神社 (千葉市花見川区)}} - Located in Hatamachi, Hanamigawa Ward, Chiba City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|ja|子安神社 (市川市)}} - Located in Kashiwaimachi, Ichikawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|ja|子安神社 (習志野市)}} - Located in Fujisaki, Narashino City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Matsudo)|ja|子安神社 (松戸市)}} - Located in Yagasaki, Matsudo City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine|ja|子安八幡神社}} - Located in Nakaiikegami and Kitakojiya, Ota Ward, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|ja|子安神社 (八王子市)}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|ja|子安神社 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|ja|子安宮澤神社}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|ja|子安八幡神社 (甲府市)}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|ja|子安神社 (河津町)}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|ja|新橋浅間神社}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|子安神社 (富士宮市)}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|ja|子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|子安神社 (浜松市東区)}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|ja|子安神社 (岐阜市)}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|ja|子安神社 (大垣市)}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|ja|子安神社 (大府市)}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|ja|子安神社 (加賀市)}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|ja|子安神社 (金沢市)}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社 (伊勢市)}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|ja|子安神社 (紀宝町)}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|ja|子安神社 (岡山市)}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社}}.

* {{ill|Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:櫻井子安神社]] - Located in Sakurai, Asahi City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Sosa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (匝瑳市)]] - Located in Yokoshiba, Sosa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hanamigawa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (千葉市花見川区)]] - Located in Hatamachi, Hanamigawa Ward, Chiba City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (市川市)]] - Located in Kashiwaimachi, Ichikawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (習志野市)]] - Located in Fujisaki, Narashino City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Matsudo)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (松戸市)]] - Located in Yagasaki, Matsudo City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安八幡神社]] - Located in Nakaiikegami and Kitakojiya, Ota Ward, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]].

[[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[ja:子安神社]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Sakurai Koyasu Shrine]]

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]].
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子守神社]] * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子育明神]] * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安地蔵]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安地蔵堂]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安地蔵寺]] * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子守地蔵]] * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子育地蔵]] * {{ill|Suitengū|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:水天宮]]

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

[[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[ja:子安神社]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Sakurai Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Pages that attribution was given to on Feb 22]][[sn:Sakurai Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Pages that attribution was given to on Feb 22]] [[sn:Sakurai Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->[[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->[[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->[[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->[[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->[[ja:吉備津彦神社]].

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}

== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}

== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website
