== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->. == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=draft}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=draft}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=draft}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->. == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Suitengū|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->

== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=draft}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.

== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->. == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Suitengū|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=draft}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=draft}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=draft}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->. == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine--> * {{ill|Suitengū|en|12=draft}} <!--Sakurai Koyasu Shrine-->

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2018-10}} {{神社 |名称=櫻井子安神社 |画像=[[File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|250px|櫻井子安神社　千葉県旭市]]
鳥居と社殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E}}}} |所在地=[[千葉県]][[旭市]]桜井1264 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木花咲耶姫命]]
[[伊邪那岐命]]
[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|250px|手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]桜井にある[[神社]]。社殿前には縁起物「赤麻紐」が中に入った赤い木の玉の「祈願玉」が置いてある。[https://spicomi.net/media/articles/3310 櫻井子安神社の徹底取材レポ！ご利益・御朱印・お守り] == 歴史 == 当神社は、宮司を務める上代家の本務社である「[[左右大神]]」の境外社として櫻井の地に鎮座しており、御祭神に[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]](このはなさくやひめのみこと)、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]](いざなぎのみこと)、[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]](いざなみのみこと)を奉齋しております。 勧請の年月は、詳らかではありませんが、極めて古くに此の地に祀られました。 古来当神社は、お祀りしている御祭神からもお分かりのとおり良縁を求める人々の縁結びのご利益、出産は勿論、なかなか子宝に恵まれない婦人の信心宿願あれば顕著な霊験があるとして、遠くからも陸続と参詣者があり、上代郷櫻井(古き土地の名称)の子安さまと尊称されていました。また、古くは櫻井子安大神と呼ばれております。 口伝によれば、当社の赤麻紐(子安さまの赤帯)は、男女の縁は固より親子の縁をも繋ぐと云われ、良縁を望む子女、子宝に恵まれない婦人は、懐中に抱くことによりその願いが叶うと伝えられています。 後に時間と共に、この赤麻紐は、良縁、子宝安産の他にも健康、学業、開運等の御利益もあるとお守りとして人々に大切にされております。 また、当神社の鎮座します地域は、[[神葬祭]](神道式の葬儀)を行う家庭もあるということから、命を授かり誕生し、[[七五三]]を経て、成長の過程での儀礼、[[厄祓い]]、健康長寿の祈願、そして実りあった一生を締めくくる[[神葬祭]]と、子安さまは、人の一生を見守ってくださる神様としても大変貴ばれております。 == 祭神 == * [[木花咲耶姫命]] * [[伊邪那岐命]] * [[伊邪那美命]] == 境内 == * 社殿 * 社務所 * 御神木 * 手水舎 * 石碑 == 行事 == * 1月1日 - 歳旦祭 * 9月 - 氏神祭 * 11月 - 例祭 * 毎月1日、15日 - 月次祭 == 交通 == JR総武本線　旭駅 → バス停萬歳小学校 下車 徒歩11分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [https://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト） {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:さくらいこやすしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

[[伊邪那岐命]]
[[伊邪那美命]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= }} [[File:櫻井子安神社 手水舎.jpg|thumb|250px|手水舎]] '''櫻井子安神社'''（さくらいこやすじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]桜井にある[[神社]]。社殿前には縁起物「赤麻紐」が中に入った赤い木の玉の「祈願玉」が置いてある。[https://spicomi.net/media/articles/3310 櫻井子安神社の徹底取材レポ！ご利益・御朱印・お守り] == 歴史 == 当神社は、宮司を務める上代家の本務社である「[[左右大神]]」の境外社として櫻井の地に鎮座しており、御祭神に[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]](このはなさくやひめのみこと)、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]](いざなぎのみこと)、[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]](いざなみのみこと)を奉齋しております。 勧請の年月は、詳らかではありませんが、極めて古くに此の地に祀られました。 古来当神社は、お祀りしている御祭神からもお分かりのとおり良縁を求める人々の縁結びのご利益、出産は勿論、なかなか子宝に恵まれない婦人の信心宿願あれば顕著な霊験があるとして、遠くからも陸続と参詣者があり、上代郷櫻井(古き土地の名称)の子安さまと尊称されていました。また、古くは櫻井子安大神と呼ばれております。 口伝によれば、当社の赤麻紐(子安さまの赤帯)は、男女の縁は固より親子の縁をも繋ぐと云われ、良縁を望む子女、子宝に恵まれない婦人は、懐中に抱くことによりその願いが叶うと伝えられています。 後に時間と共に、この赤麻紐は、良縁、子宝安産の他にも健康、学業、開運等の御利益もあるとお守りとして人々に大切にされております。 また、当神社の鎮座します地域は、[[神葬祭]](神道式の葬儀)を行う家庭もあるということから、命を授かり誕生し、[[七五三]]を経て、成長の過程での儀礼、[[厄祓い]]、健康長寿の祈願、そして実りあった一生を締めくくる[[神葬祭]]と、子安さまは、人の一生を見守ってくださる神様としても大変貴ばれております。 == 祭神 == * [[木花咲耶姫命]] * [[伊邪那岐命]] * [[伊邪那美命]] == 境内 == * 社殿 * 社務所 * 御神木 * 手水舎 * 石碑 == 行事 == * 1月1日 - 歳旦祭 * 9月 - 氏神祭 * 11月 - 例祭 * 毎月1日、15日 - 月次祭 == 交通 == JR総武本線　旭駅 → バス停萬歳小学校 下車 徒歩11分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == == 外部リンク == [https://www.kajiro.jp/ 櫻井子安神社]（公式サイト） {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:さくらいこやすしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]]
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|qid=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20044508]]
Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|qq=|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

== References ==
{{Notes help}}
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}

== External Links ==
* [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website

 - Located in Sakurai, Asahi City, Chiba Prefecture.

* {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|qq=}}
 - Located in Kashiwaimachi, Ichikawa City, Chiba Prefecture.
* {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]]
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20044508]]
Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|qq=|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|qq=|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]]
Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]]
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]]
Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

* Stone Monument

* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]]
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]]
Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]]
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]]
Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]]
Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]]
