{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]]
Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]] Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]], to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website
{{coord|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=title|name=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}
* {{ill|Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} - Located in Sakurai, Asahi City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Sosa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Sosa)|qq=}} - Located in Yokoshiba, Sosa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hanamigawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Hanamigawa)|qq=}} - Located in Hatamachi, Hanamigawa Ward, Chiba City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|qq=}} - Located in Kashiwaimachi, Ichikawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|qq=}} - Located in Fujisaki, Narashino City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Matsudo)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Matsudo)|qq=}} - Located in Yagasaki, Matsudo City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} - Located in Nakaiikegami and Kitakojiya, Ota Ward, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|qq=}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|qq=}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasumiya Shrine|qq=}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|qq=}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|qq=}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|qq=}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|qq=}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|qq=}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|qq=}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|qq=}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|qq=}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|qq=}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu Shrine|qq=}}

The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044508]] Wikidata name: Sakurai koyasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}} '''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.
== History == The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).
The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.
According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
== Grounds == * Main Shrine * Shrine Office * Sacred Tree * Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monument
== Festivals == * January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival) * September - Ujigami Festival * November - Annual Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)
== Access == From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
== References == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kajiro.jp/ Sakurai Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website
{{coord|35|47|8.1|N|140|38|41.8|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=title|name=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}
* {{ill|Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|qq=}} - Located in Sakurai, Asahi City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Sosa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Sosa)|qq=}} - Located in Yokoshiba, Sosa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hanamigawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Hanamigawa)|qq=}} - Located in Hatamachi, Hanamigawa Ward, Chiba City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|qq=}} - Located in Kashiwaimachi, Ichikawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|qq=}} - Located in Fujisaki, Narashino City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Matsudo)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Matsudo)|qq=}} - Located in Yagasaki, Matsudo City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} - Located in Nakaiikegami and Kitakojiya, Ota Ward, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|qq=}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|qq=}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasumiya Shrine|qq=}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|qq=}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|qq=}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|qq=}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|qq=}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|qq=}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|qq=}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|qq=}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|qq=}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|qq=}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu Shrine|qq=}}

The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.
In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫻井子安神社]]
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

== References ==
{{Notes help}}
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}

* {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|qq=}}

== Kinki Region ==
* {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}
 - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868|1=Asahi, Chiba}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306|1=Izanami}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868|1=Asahi, Chiba}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306|1=Izanami}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==

* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==

* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:櫻井子安神社.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Asahi|WD=Q637868|1=Asahi, Chiba}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.785583|N|140.644944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306|1=Izanami}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}}}
{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.

{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=8.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=38|lon_sec=41.8|label=Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=220|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Shinto shrine).svg|marksize=12|caption={{Center|Location of Sakurai Koyasu Shrine}}}}
'''Sakurai Koyasu Shrine''' (櫻井子安神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The shrine serves as an external shrine of the {{ill|Sayou Daijin|ja|左右大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayou Daijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左右神社|1=Sayū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282615}}, overseen by the Kamishiro family. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Konoha-no-sakuya-hime-no-mikoto), {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-mikoto), and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-mikoto).

The exact date of its establishment is unknown, but it has been worshiped in this area since ancient times. Historically, the shrine has been revered as "Koyasu-sama of Sakurai" due to its blessings for good relationships, childbirth, and fertility. It was also known as "Sakurai Koyasu Daijin" in the past.

According to oral tradition, the shrine's red hemp cord (Koyasu-sama's red belt) symbolizes connections not only between couples but also between parents and children. It is believed that carrying the cord can fulfill wishes related to good relationships and fertility. Over time, it has also become a charm for health, academic success, and good fortune.

In addition, as some households in the region perform {{ill|Shinsōsai|ja|神葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsōsai|1=Shinto funeral|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590783}} (Shinto-style funerals), the shrine is respected as a guardian deity overseeing the entire life cycle—from birth, through rites of passage like {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}}, to funerals.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}

== Grounds ==
* Main Shrine
* Shrine Office
* Sacred Tree
* Chōzusha (Ablution Pavilion)
* Stone Monument

== Festivals ==
* January 1 - Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)
* September - Ujigami Festival
* November - Annual Festival
* 1st and 15th of every month - Tsukinami-sai (Monthly Festival)

== Access ==
From JR Sōbu Main Line, get off at Asahi Station, then walk 11 minutes from the Banzai Elementary School bus stop.
